title: cordylanthus mollis subsp. hispidus
intro: Cordylanthus mollis subsp. hispidus, also known as the rough-leaved bird's-beak or rough bird's-beak, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) that is native to the Pacific coast of North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness, as well as to support the immune system and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, Cordylanthus mollis subsp. hispidus is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its health benefits, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote calmness and well-being.
